---
title: "Question 2 - One-way ANOVA (Oil filling machine efficiency)"
author: "Upayan Mazumder"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
alpha <- 0.05
```

## Question

Test equality of efficiency of filling machines A, B, C, D and E using the sampled tin weights.

```{r data}
machine_data <- list(
  A = c(14.85, 15.00, 15.25, 15.10, 14.80),
  B = c(14.28, 14.42),
  C = c(14.16, 14.15, 14.19, 14.50),
  D = c(15.25, 15.30, 15.10, 15.35, 15.00),
  E = c(14.60, 14.84, 14.82, 14.74)
)

df <- stack(machine_data)
names(df) <- c("Weight", "Machine")
df$Machine <- factor(df$Machine)

df
aggregate(Weight ~ Machine, data = df, FUN = mean)
```

## One-way ANOVA

```{r anova}
fit <- aov(Weight ~ Machine, data = df)
anova_table <- summary(fit)
anova_table
```

```{r assumptions}
shapiro.test(residuals(fit))
bartlett.test(Weight ~ Machine, data = df)
```

```{r posthoc}
TukeyHSD(fit)
```

```{r decision}
p_machine <- anova_table[[1]]["Machine", "Pr(>F)"]

if (p_machine < alpha) {
  cat("Decision: Reject H0 at 5% significance level.\n")
  cat("Conclusion: Machine means are not all equal; efficiency differs significantly.")
} else {
  cat("Decision: Fail to reject H0 at 5% significance level.\n")
  cat("Conclusion: No significant difference in mean efficiency is detected across machines.")
}
```
